messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] LCMATE mailing list
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

 

We have created LCMATE (Latent Class Models for the Accuracy of TEsts), a
mailing list for people that are interested in the development and
application of latent class models for the evaluation of diagnostic tests,
primarily within a Bayesian framework. The focus is on estimating the
diagnostic accuracy of tests (either dichotomous, ordinal or continuous) in
the absence of a gold standard. LCMs are used within this context because
the true disease status of the individuals/samples is unknown (i.e. latent,
or hidden).

 

The scope of this list is to:

1. Assist researchers that are interested in the application of these
methods to their own data

2. Promote the exchange of knowledge and ideas between researchers working
in this area

 

To subscribe please visit:

 

http://lists.uth.gr/mailman/listinfo/lcmate

 

or send an email to:

 

lcmate-request at lists.uth.gr

 

Kind Regards,

Polychronis Kostoulas

LCMATE Administrator

 


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] R-sig-Epi Digest, Vol 90, Issue 1
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
I am glad to be part of the list.

I am collaborating with academics in the States from the Harvard Medical School evaluating the performance (diagnosis accuracy) of a new biomarker for the diagnosis of Gestational Diabetes Mellitus.
http://care.diabetesjournals.org/content/early/2017/04/05/dc16-2598
However, the gold standard is imperfect (Oral glucose tolerance test), and we cannot get correct estimates of the AUC for the new biomarker.
Also, I thought about the evaluation of the performance in and independent sample size, which is not the standard case in medical studies, using cross-validation to adjust the value of the AUC
https://github.com/migariane/cvAUROC
However, I will be glad if any of you can refer to some seminal papers/references (in addition to the Pepe book) to illustrate how to implement the LCMATE analysis.
Thank you in advance for your time and help.
Kind regards,

Miguel Angel Luque Fernandez, PhD
Assistant Professor of Epidemiology and Biostatistics
London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine
London, UK



-----Original Message-----
From: R-sig-Epi [mailto:r-sig-epi-bounces at r-project.org] On Behalf Of r-sig-epi-request at r-project.org
Sent: 04 May 2017 11:00
To: r-sig-epi at r-project.org
Subject: R-sig-Epi Digest, Vol 90, Issue 1

Send R-sig-Epi mailing list submissions to
	r-sig-epi at r-project.org

To subscribe or unsubscribe via the World Wide Web, visit
	https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-epi
or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to
	r-sig-epi-request at r-project.org

You can reach the person managing the list at
	r-sig-epi-owner at r-project.org

When replying, please edit your Subject line so it is more specific than "Re: Contents of R-sig-Epi digest..."


Today's Topics:

   1. LCMATE mailing list (Polychronis Kostoulas)

Message-ID: <000001d2c413$814fd080$83ef7180$@vet.uth.gr>
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="UTF-8"

Dear colleagues,



We have created LCMATE (Latent Class Models for the Accuracy of TEsts), a mailing list for people that are interested in the development and application of latent class models for the evaluation of diagnostic tests, primarily within a Bayesian framework. The focus is on estimating the diagnostic accuracy of tests (either dichotomous, ordinal or continuous) in the absence of a gold standard. LCMs are used within this context because the true disease status of the individuals/samples is unknown (i.e. latent, or hidden).



The scope of this list is to:

1. Assist researchers that are interested in the application of these methods to their own data

2. Promote the exchange of knowledge and ideas between researchers working in this area



To subscribe please visit:



http://lists.uth.gr/mailman/listinfo/lcmate



or send an email to:



lcmate-request at lists.uth.gr



Kind Regards,

Polychronis Kostoulas

LCMATE Administrator

messages.subject: [R-sig-Epi] surv function type argument. How to set it
messages.subject_clean: surv function type argument. How to set it
messages.body_plain:
good morning,
I'm not sure how to set the "type" argument correctly in the "Surv"
function ("survival" package) for my data.
In our survival study, a group of oysters is checked at irregular time
intervals.
At each timestep, the number of death, scattered and still alive oysters
has been recorded. Following an example data.frame:
Time1 <-rep (c (0.200, 300), each = 3)
Time2 <-rep (c (200, 300, 370), each = 3)
Event_death <-c (10,8,15)
Event_scattered_censored <-c (3,5,7)
data <-data.frame (Time1, Time2, Event_death, Event_scattered_censored)
data $ Still_alive <-1000-cumsum (Event_scattered_censored + Event_death)

I do not understand how this kind of data can be correctly used by the
"Surv" function and how to set the corresponding type argument.
Thanks for the help, greetings, luca

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
